description: Executor contracts for Agent, TypeScript code, nested workflow, adapter, and human nodes, with Agent nodes routed through normal Pibo Runtime and strict policy boundaries.

userStories.title: Implement shared node executor dispatch contract
userStories.description: As a workflow author, I want all V1 node kinds supported through a common attempt model so that execution is inspectable.
acceptanceCriteria: Runtime dispatches node kinds agent, code, workflow, adapter, and human
Each executor validates input before work and output after work
Each executor returns structured result, commands, patches, or diagnostics through the shared node attempt model
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement Agent executor through normal Pibo Runtime
userStories.description: As a workflow author, I want Agent nodes to use pibo-agent or another fixed Agent Designer profile so that behavior matches normal sessions.
acceptanceCriteria: Agent node requires runtime pibo and fixed profile selection in V1
Executor resolves fixed Agent Designer profile and rejects unknown profile
Executor creates or attaches to a routed Pibo Session through normal Pibo session routing
Executor records piboSessionId, optional piSessionId, trace links, effective profile, tools, skills, context, and routing metadata
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement Agent prompt construction and structured output validation
userStories.description: As an agent profile owner, I want prompts and outputs handled consistently so that Agent nodes remain policy-bound.
acceptanceCriteria: Agent executor builds final prompt from promptTemplate, promptBuilder, or prompt asset
Fixed prompts and prompt builders both record final prompts subject to existing trace/privacy rules
Agent executor collects text or structured output and validates declared output port before completion/downstream transfer
Capability validation checks structured-output support or declared fallback when possible
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement registered TypeScript code executor
userStories.description: As a developer, I want code nodes to call registered handlers so that logic is testable and not arbitrary hidden code.
acceptanceCriteria: Code node uses language typescript and a registered handler id
Handler receives scoped context with input, global, local, edge, emit, and command capabilities
Executor enforces declared reads/writes and rejects undeclared state writes
Inline code and arbitrary shell/script nodes are not implemented as V1 primitives
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement nested workflow executor
userStories.description: As a workflow author, I want nested workflows so that reusable workflows compose without sharing internal state by default.
acceptanceCriteria: Nested executor resolves child workflow id/version and creates a child workflow run
Child input is validated against child input port and child output maps to parent node output
Parent/child run ids, checkpoint namespace, and events are linked
Child global/local state stays isolated unless explicit export is configured
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement adapter executor and edge adapter attempts
userStories.description: As a developer, I want deterministic adapters so that interface transformations are visible and testable.
acceptanceCriteria: Adapter nodes and edge adapters resolve registered TypeScript adapter handlers
Executor validates source payload, runs deterministic transform, validates adapter output against declared output and target input, and persists adapter attempt/errors
Hidden agent-assisted adapters are not implemented in V1
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Integrate Human executor with durable waits
userStories.description: As a reviewer, I want human nodes to pause reliably so that actions can resume workflows later.
acceptanceCriteria: Human executor creates durable wait tokens through the runtime kernel before returning control
Human executor exposes available actions and validates resume/action payloads
Human executor routes approval, rejection, timeout, cancellation, invalid resume, and normal resume through declared edges when present
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Enforce environment and capability policies for executors
userStories.description: As a security reviewer, I want node execution to obey Pibo boundaries so that workflows do not grant extra power.
acceptanceCriteria: Environment policy supports inherit, host, worktree, docker-worker, and remote with default inherit
Effective environment is stored on run or node attempt and passed through scoped executor context
Capability validation checks profile existence, tool/skill/context availability, session resume, timeout, budget, isolation, environment policy, and known concurrency limits when possible
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
